description: Machine-readable form of the LOCKED Entity-Graph Ontology v2. Single source of truth (Going Meta principle 1): drives the builders, the LLM-extraction prompt, and the generic domain/range invariant in data/validate_kg.py. Closed vocabulary: any node kind / edge type / atom type / op used in kg/*.json MUST be declared here (live, reserved, or legacy) or it is a VIOLATION. Changes are ADDITIVE (new kind/edge/atom) via link-don't-merge, never a re-ingest.

conventions.wildcard: A domain or range value of "*" means any declared node kind (the v2 'any' relation).

status_values.live: Has data in the committed graph today or in the imminent build; domain/range is enforced.
status_values.reserved: Declared by the v2 spec but not yet populated (decision 8 / NICE). Reserves the slot; enforced once populated.
status_values.legacy: A v1 kind/atom physically present in the committed graph but NOT part of the v2 ontology. Tolerated for byte-stable, link-don't-merge migration; each names its v2 successor. Usage is reported at INFO severity (tracked, non-blocking), never VIOLATION. Empties as nodes/edges migrate.

dst_policy.required: Edge must carry a non-null dst.
dst_policy.optional: Edge may carry a dst or be a pure-condition / dst=None edge (the constraint IS the cond_group, or the payload rides in data).
dst_policy.forbidden: Edge must not carry a dst.

conventions.cond_group_policy: Documentation of whether an edge typically carries a requires/recommended_for cond_group. Recorded for the builders and the LLM-extraction prompt; NOT enforced as a VIOLATION in this build step (domain/range + closed-vocabulary are the enforced invariants).

severity_tiers.VIOLATION: Hard failure. validate_kg exits non-zero. True schema breach: undeclared kind/edge/atom/op, or a domain/range mismatch on a known edge.
severity_tiers.WARNING: Tracked, non-fatal. Lands as a notice (e.g. disclosed known-gap / mis-filed record). Does not affect exit code.
severity_tiers.INFO: Informational, non-fatal. Migration/coverage signal (e.g. legacy-kind usage, reserved-kind population). Does not affect exit code.

decisions.1: 'Where' is THREE relations: located_in (containment tree) + rule_applies_in (overlapping typed overlay) + a coordinate field (OSRS chunks).
decisions.2: Interactable kinds = npc | monster | scenery, each carrying a role attribute.
decisions.3: Relationship edges are REIFIED (data-carrying) for drops/sells/recipe/grants; shops two-way + currency-typed.
decisions.4: Requirement kinds = hard requires + soft recommended_for (advisory, strength enum, never blocks).
decisions.5: Identity = cache-ids canonical + item page-identity with variant children.
decisions.6: Aliases = typed aliases[] on nodes (wiki-redirect-sourced) + a search-resolution layer for ambiguous terms.
decisions.7: gate_type on hard requires: open verb set access|damage|kill (combat) + make|craft|... via the recipe's requires/consumes.
decisions.8: Context axes game_mode, real-time clock, difficulty_config defined NOW (reserve slots), populated later.
decisions.9: Competency-questions CI gate ON from day one.

place.notes: Recursive containment node (decision 1). Geometry = a chunk-set. Supersedes the v1 'region' kind.

monster.notes: Attackable interactable (decision 2). Carries a role; combat stats, weakness, immunities, slayer category.

item.notes: Page-identity + variant children (decision 5): each variant is a cache item_id bridged to the page by same_entity; reified edges target the variant. Page-identity nodes are slug-keyed (item:<page-slug>) and family nodes item:<family-slug>; variant nodes are cache-id-keyed.

currency.notes: coins is just one (tokkul, commendation/slayer points, marks, tickets). Prices reference a currency_ref.

quest.notes: requires DAG from Module:Questreq/data (3-state + step, ironman/boostable flags) + has_part subquests.

diary.notes: Achievement-diary tier node. Carries region + tier; covers->places.

goal.notes: Completion-goal aggregate (Quest cape, music cape, ...). data={counter_type, thresholds}.

transport_system.notes: Shared-mechanic system node owning the base requires once (decision 7 / §7). Tier = transport_tier sub-entity.

activity.notes: An activity/content node (effect targets, drop sources).

equipment_bonuses.notes: The gear/BIS substrate facet (has_bonuses). Supersedes part of the v1 'gear_loadout' kind.

item_set.notes: Named item set (member_of). Supersedes part of the v1 'gear_loadout' kind.

set_effect.notes: The bonus an item_set confers when complete.

subquest.notes: Composite-quest part (has_part). NOTE: today's composite quests (e.g. RFD) are modeled as quest: nodes; this kind reserves the explicit successor slot.

rule_zone.notes: Typed, overlapping, world-spanning overlay (rule_applies_in / decision 1 / §5): multicombat | pvp | no_teleport | safe. Each instance = a chunk-set.

game_mode.notes: Context axis (decision 8). Atoms can gate on game_mode == X.

difficulty_config.notes: Typed difficulty_input (decision 8 / pass-3): binary_flag | scalar_toggle_sum | accumulating_draft | floor_index | party_size; elected?. scales_with the encounter.

equipment_group.notes: Equipment grouping (member_of). Successor to part of the v1 'gear_loadout'.

region.notes: v1 geographic container. Migrate to 'place' (place_type=region/city/...) with chunk geometry. Present in committed graph; targeted by effect edges (the 'effect rides on a regional item' substrate).

access.successor: place (reached via gives_access / served_by / connects_to edges)
access.notes: v1 abstract unlock node (e.g. access:fairy-rings). v2 models reachability as transport/scenery edges into a place, gated by a cond_group; no standalone 'access' kind.

gear_loadout.notes: v1 named gear composition (one dst=None requires edge = its members). v2 splits into item_set/equipment_group membership + the equipment_bonuses facet.

account_type.notes: v1 NodeKind enum value with no instances in the committed graph. v2 expresses account family as the account_type CONDITION ATOM (and game_mode context, decision 8), not a standalone node. Declared legacy so a vestigial builder emitting one lands INFO, not a spurious VIOLATION.

clog_slot.notes: v1 NodeKind enum value with no instances in the committed graph. v2 expresses collection-log entries as item membership + clog/aggregate atoms. Declared legacy for model-enum coverage.

gated_by.successor: requires (the gate IS the requires edge's cond_group)
gated_by.notes: v1 EdgeType enum value with no instances in the committed graph and no v2 edge-table row. v2 folds gating into the requires edge's cond_group (D5). Declared legacy so it lands INFO, not a VIOLATION, and so the schema is a complete superset of the model EdgeType enum.

requires.notes: Hard gate; drives met/blocked (decision 4). Carries gate_type (decision 7). dst is normally None (the constraint IS the cond_group); a few requires edges point directly at a node (e.g. goal->goal composition).

grants.notes: Reified reward payload (decision 3). dst=None for a bundle described purely in data.reward; skill/access ranges are v1-era XP-lamp / unlock grants pending the currency/place model.

effect.notes: Context-scoped effect (§3 effect | applies_when). Today every effect rides on an item (src=item) targeting a content node; the per-edge condition lives in data (data.condition / applies_when), not a cond_group. 'region' in range is the legacy target of 'place'. dst=None = a quest-era perk with no content target.

progress_towards.notes: Counter contribution toward a goal node. data.weight (positive int).

located_in.notes: Containment TREE (decision 1): world > kingdom > city > district > scenery.

rule_applies_in.notes: Overlapping typed overlay (decision 1 / §5), world-spanning; each instance = a chunk-set.

sells.notes: Reified: {price, currency, stock, restock} + optional gate (decision 3).

drops.notes: Reified {rate, rolls, drop_type, version}. NEVER fabricate the loot MECHANISM (RDT/GDT) — record flat observable rates (Option-A).

recommended_for.notes: SOFT advisory; never blocks (decision 4). Carries strength in {required_for_survival, strongly_recommended, optional}. Uses the FULL atom vocabulary (recommended combat stats/levels for bosses/raids).

connects_to.notes: Transport link with its own cond_group (§7).

scales_with.notes: Typed difficulty input -> encounter stat-block + difficulty-conditional drop_table (decision 8 / pass-3).

immune_to.notes: Negative relation: monster <-> damage-type/gear (damage-type not yet a node kind).

counters.notes: Negative relation (spec: monster <-> damage-type/gear). 'monster' in domain is an intentional symmetric extension (monster-counters-monster).

realizable_via.notes: Iron-realizable exit tracing: currency -> bridge item.

service.notes: repair/toll/lost-item; cost+cond. Range is an effect target (effect not yet a node kind).

instance_of.notes: place x type instances; item-variant -> type. The spec's abstract range 'type' is concretized here as place/item/facility.

quest.notes: 3-state ORDERED enum (not_started < in_progress < completed) + optional step (decision 7).

combat_achievement_points.notes: Accumulator tier total (points-based tier). Deliberately split from per-task combat_achievement.

aggregate_count.notes: Cardinality of completed members of a set (successor to count_satisfied).

wilderness_level.notes: Coordinate threshold derived from chunk position (§5).

all_of.notes: Quantified over a set (every member satisfied).

is_unlocked.notes: v1 derived/structural atom; v2 composes the requires closure instead. Not stored in any committed condition_group.

count_satisfied.notes: v1 cardinality-of-completed-set atom (goal:*-cape). Same shape as aggregate_count.

gear_loadout.notes: v1 'loadout composition satisfied' atom. v2 expresses the same via a quantified all_of over the set's item/equipped atoms.

scope_lifetime.notes: Single highest-leverage tag (§4): prevents false 'you can do X permanently' claims (on-task, killcount, teleblock, random-event clocks). Default = permanent.

gate_type.notes: On hard requires (decision 7). Production verbs are structured by recipe requires(=capability) vs consumes(=resource). Open set.

game_mode.notes: Scope flag (decision 8): atoms can gate on game_mode == X like account_type.

vocab.drop_type_note: Observable rate only (Option-A). The loot MECHANISM (RDT/GDT vs direct vs on-task) is NOT fabricated.
